*   **"weni elisathuthuka"**: Translated as "developing country."

translations.source: Isifo sofuba Uhlolojikelele: iTB ibangelwa amagciwane (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) futhi ngokuvamile ihlasela amaphaphu. iTB isakazwa ngomoya lapho abantu abaneTB yamaphaphu bekhwehlela, bethimula noma bekhafula. Umuntu udinga ukuhogela amagciwane ambalwa ukuze angenwe yilesi sifo. Njalo ngonyaka, abantu abayizigidi eziyi-10 baphathwa isifo sofuba (TB).
translations.reference: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB).
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

**Tuberculosis (TB) Overview:** TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and primarily attacks the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with pulmonary TB cough, sneeze, or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few bacteria to become infected with this disease. Every year, approximately 10 million people are treated for tuberculosis (TB).

translations.source: Nakuba kuyisifo esingagwemeka futhi eselaphekayo, abantu abayizigidi ezingu-1.5 babulawa iTB unyaka ngamunye – okuyenza ibe umbulali ohamba phambili emhlabeni wonke. iTB iyimbangela ehamba phambili yokufa kwabantu abane-HIV futhi iphinde ibe neqhaza elikhulu ekwandeni kwemithi elwa namagciwane Iningi labantu abaguliswa yiTB lihlala emazweni anemali ephansi noma ephakathi, kodwa iTB ikhona emhlabeni wonke. Cishe ingxenye yabo bonke abantu abaneTB bangatholakala emazweni angu-8: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines naseNingizimu Afrika. Cishe ingxenye yesine yabantu emhlabeni wonke kulinganiselwa ukuthi inamagciwane eTB, kodwa abantu abaningi ngeke baqhubeke nokuba nesifo sofuba futhi abanye bazosiqeda lesi sifo.
translations.reference: Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

"Although tuberculosis (TB) is a curable and preventable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the leading killer worldwide. TB is a leading cause of death among people with HIV and also contributes significantly to the rise of antimicrobial resistance. The majority of people affected by TB live in low- or middle-income countries, but TB exists worldwide. Approximately one-third of all people with TB can be found in just 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines, and South Africa. Roughly one-quarter of the world's population is estimated to have a latent TB infection, but most people will not go on to develop active TB disease, and some will clear the infection."

translations.source: Labo abangenwe yilesi sifo kodwa abangakaguli (okwamanje) abanalesi sifo abakwazi ukusidlulisela. Abantu abangenwe amagciwane eTB basengozini engu-5-10% yokuphila konke yokugula iTB. Labo abanamasosha omzimba asengozini, njengabantu abaphila neHIV, abangondleki kahle noma abanesifo sikashukela, noma abantu abasebenzisa ugwayi, basengozini enkulu yokugula. Izimpawu: Izimpawu ezijwayelekile zesifo sofuba zihlanganisa: Ukukhwehlela isikhathi eside, ubuhlungu besifuba, ubuthakathaka noma ukukhathala, ukuncipha komzimba, Imfiva, Ukujuluka ebusuku. Ngokuvamile, lezi zimpawu zizoba mnene izinyanga eziningi, ngaleyo ndlela kuholele ekubambezelekeni kokufuna ukunakekelwa futhi kwandise ingozi yokusabalalisa ukutheleleka kwabanye.
translations.reference: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

"Those who have not been vaccinated against this disease but who are not currently known to have the disease can transmit it. People who have not been infected with TB germs are at a 5-10% lifetime risk of developing TB. Those with weakened immune systems, such as people living with HIV, who are malnourished, have diabetes, or use tobacco, are at higher risk of contracting the disease. Symptoms: Common symptoms of tuberculosis include: prolonged coughing, chest pain, weakness or fatigue, weight loss, fever, and night sweats. Typically, these symptoms will worsen over many months, leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others."

translations.source: Uma umhlinzeki wezempilo esola ukuthi isiguli sinesifo sofuba, bazothumela isiguli ukuthi siyohlolwa. Esimeni sokusolwa kwesifo sofuba samaphaphu, iziguli zizocelwa ukuthi zinikeze isampula yesikhwehlela ukuze kuhlolelwe amagciwane eTB. Ngesifo sofuba esingesona esamaphaphu, amasampula oketshezi lomzimba oluthintekile kanye nezicubu angahlolwa. IWHO incoma ukuhlolwa kokuxilonga kwamangqamuzana okusheshayo njengokuhlola kokuqala kwabantu ababonisa izimpawu nezimpawu zeTB. Amanye amathuluzi okuxilonga angabandakanya iSputum smear microscopy nama-X-ray esifuba.
translations.reference: If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria. For non-lung TB disease, samples of affected body fluids and tissue can be tested. WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as initial tests for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools can include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

"When a healthcare provider suspects a patient has tuberculosis (TB), they will refer the patient for testing. In the case of suspected pulmonary TB, patients will be asked to provide a sputum sample to check for TB germs. For non-pulmonary TB, samples of body fluids and tissues may be examined. The WHO recommends rapid diagnostic testing (CD4 count testing) as the initial test for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools may include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays."

translations.source: Ngokungenwa yiTB, umuntu ungenwa amagciwane eTB alele engasebenzi emzimbeni. Lesi sifo singakhula sibe yisifo sofuba uma amasosha omzimba eba buthaka. Abantu abaneTB ababonisi zimpawu noma izimpawu zeTB. Ukuhlonza ukutheleleka ngeTB, abahlinzeki bezempilo bazohlola iziguli ezisengozini ukuze bakhiphe iTB ephilayo, futhi bangasebenzisa isikhumba noma ukuhlolwa kwegazi ukuhlola ukuthi ayinalo yini iTB. Ukwelashwa: Isifo sofuba siyelapheka.
translations.reference: With TB infection, a person gets infected with TB bacteria that lie inactive in the body. This infection can develop into TB disease if their immune system weakens. People with TB infection do not show any signs or symptoms of TB. To identify TB infection, healthcare providers will screen at-risk patients to rule out active TB, and they may use a skin or blood test to check for TB infection. Treatment: TB disease is curable.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu text:

Without active TB, a person is not infected with TB germs that are not working in the body. This disease can develop into tuberculosis if the body's defenses weaken. People with TB may not show symptoms or may have symptoms of TB. To screen for TB infection, healthcare providers will test patients at risk to detect living TB, and may use skin or blood tests to check for TB. Treatment: Tuberculosis is curable.

translations.source: Yelashwa ngenkambo evamile yezinyanga eziyi-6 yama-antibiotic ama-4. Imithi ejwayelekile ihlanganisa irifampicin ne-isoniazid. Kwezinye izimo amagciwane eTB awaphenduli emithini evamile. Kulokhu, isiguli sineTB engazweli emishanguzweni. kuTB engazweli emishanguzweni yinde futhi iyinkimbinkimbi.
translations.reference: It is treated by standard 6 month course of 4 antibiotics. Common drugs include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases the TB bacteria does not respond to the standard drugs. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. for drug-resistant TB is longer and more complex.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu text:

"The patient was treated with a standard 6-month course of 4 antibiotics. Standard treatment includes rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases, TB germs do not respond to standard medications. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. Drug-resistant TB is long and complex."

translations.source: Imithi yeTB inikezwa isiguli ngolwazi, ukugadwa nokusekelwa umsebenzi wezempilo noma ivolontiya eliqeqeshiwe. Ngaphandle kokusekelwa okunjalo, ukubambelela ekwelashweni kungaba nzima. Uma ukwelashwa kungaqedwa kahle, lesi sifo singamelana nemithi futhi singasakazeka. Esimeni sokutheleleka ngeTB (lapho isiguli sitheleleke ngamagciwane eTB kodwa singaguli), ukwelashwa kokuvimbela iTB kungenziwa ukuze kunqandwe ukuqala kwesifo. Lokhu kwelashwa kusebenzisa izidakamizwa ezifanayo isikhathi esifushane.
translations.reference: The course of TB drugs is provided to the patient with information, supervision and support by a health worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult. If the treatment is not properly completed, the disease can become drug-resistant and can spread. In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill), TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease. This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu text:

"TB treatment is provided to patients with knowledge, examination, and support from a healthcare worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, adherence to treatment can be difficult. If treatment is completed properly, this disease can become resistant to medication and continue to spread. In the case of TB infection (where a patient is infected with TB germs but is not sick), preventative TB treatment can be done to prevent the onset of the disease. This treatment uses the same medications for a shorter period."

translations.source: Izinketho zokwelashwa zakamuva zifinyeze ubude besikhathi sokwelashwa saba yizinyanga ezi-1 noma ezi-3 kuphela, uma kuqhathaniswa nezinyanga ezingu-6 ezedlule.
translations.reference: Recent treatment options have shortened the duration to treatment to only 1 or 3 months, as compared to 6 months in the past.
translations.hypothesis: Recent treatment options have reduced the duration of treatment to just 1 or 3 months, compared to the previous 6 months.

translations.source: Imisebe yeUltraviolet Uhlolojikelele: Imisebe ye-Ultraviolet (UV) ivala ububanzi be-wavelength obungu-100–400 nm, okuyifrikhwensi ephezulu kanye nobude obuphansi begagasi kunokukhanya okubonakalayo. Imisebe ye-UV ivela ngokwemvelo ivela elangeni, kodwa ingabuye idalwe ngemithombo yokwenziwa esetshenziswa embonini, ezentengiselwano nezokuzijabulisa. Isifunda se-UV simboza ububanzi be-wavelength 100-400 nm futhi sihlukaniswe ngamabhande amathathu: UVA (315-400 nm) UVB (280-315 nm) UVC (100-280 nm). Njengoba ukukhanya kwelanga kudlula emkhathini, yonke i-UVC kanye nama-90% emisebe ye-UVB amuncwa i-ozone, umhwamuko wamanzi, umoyampilo kanye nesikhutha.
translations.reference: Ultraviolet radiation Overview: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers the wavelength range of 100–400 nm, which is a higher frequency and lower wavelength than visible light. UV radiation comes naturally from the sun, but it can also be created by artificial sources used in industry, commerce and recreation. The UV region covers the wavelength range 100-400 nm and is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm)UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, all UVC and approximately 90% of UVB radiation is absorbed by ozone, water vapour, oxygen and carbon dioxide.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu text:

**Ultraviolet Spectrum: Overview:** Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers a wavelength range of 100–400 nm, a higher frequency and shorter wavelength than visible light. UV radiation occurs naturally from the sun, but can also be produced by artificial sources used in industry, commerce, and entertainment. The UV region covers a wavelength range of 100-400 nm and is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm), UVB (280-315 nm), UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, all UVC and 90% of UVB radiation are absorbed by ozone, water vapor, atmospheric moisture, and dust.

translations.source: Imisebe ye-UVA ayithinteki kangako emkhathini. Ngakho ke, imisebe ye-UV efinyelela ebusweni bomhlaba ikakhulukazi yakhiwe i-UVA enengxenye encane ye-UVB. Inani lemisebe ye-UV evela elangeni elishaya ebusweni boMhlaba lincike ezintweni ezimbalwa, okuhlanganisa ubude belanga esibhakabhakeni, i-latitude, ukumboza kwamafu, ukuphakama, ukujiya kongqimba lwe-ozone kanye nokubonakala komhlabathi. Ukwehliswa kongqimba lwe-ozone ngenxa yokungcola okudalwe umuntu kwandisa inani le-UVA ne-UVB elifika phezulu. Lokhu kungaba nomthelela empilweni yomuntu, izilwane, izinto eziphilayo zasolwandle kanye nempilo yezitshalo.
translations.reference: UVA radiation is less affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, the UV radiation reaching the Earth’s surface is largely composed of UVA with a small UVB component. The amount of UV radiation from the sun that hits the Earth’s surface depends on several factors, including the sun’s height in the sky, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer and ground reflection. Reductions in the ozone layer due to human-created pollution increase the amount of UVA and UVB that reaches the surface. This can impact human health, animals, marine organisms and plant life.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu text:

"UVA rays are not as affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, UV rays reaching the Earth's surface are primarily UVA with a small component of UVB. The amount of UV rays coming from the sun hitting the Earth's surface depends on a few things, including the length of daylight in the sky, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer, and the reflectivity of the Earth. Depletion of the ozone layer due to human-caused pollution increases the amount of UVA and UVB reaching the surface. This can have an impact on human health, animals, marine life, and plant life."

translations.source: Kubantu, ukwanda kokuchayeka kwe-UV kungabangela umdlavuza wesikhumba, ulwelwesi lwamabala kanye nokulimala kwamasosha omzimba. Ukuvimbela: Ukukhuphuka kwenani leziguli zomdlavuza wesikhumba emashumini eminyaka adlule kuhlobene kakhulu nokuchayeka elangeni ngesikhathi semisebenzi yangaphandle kanye nemithombo yokwenziwa yemisebe ye-UV efana nezibani zelanga nemibhede yokushuka. Ukuchayeka ngokweqile nakho kuyimbangela eyinhloko yemiphumela eyingozi emehlweni namasosha omzimba. Ukwamukela izinyathelo ezimbalwa ezilula zokuphepha kunganciphisa kakhulu ingozi yalezi zimo zempilo: Nciphisa isikhathi elangeni lasemini kusukela ngo-10 ekuseni kuya ku-4 ntambama, ikakhulukazi ngezinsuku lapho inkomba ye-UV iphezulu. Lapho imisebe ye-UV inamandla kakhulu futhi kufanele ube ngaphandle, zama ukuthola umthunzi futhi ugqoke izingubo zokuzivikela, kuhlanganise nesigqoko esinomphetho esinikeza isivikelo elangeni ekhanda lakho, ebusweni nasentanyeni.
translations.reference: In humans, increased UV exposure can cause skin cancers, cataracts and immune system damage. Prevention: The rise in the number of cases of skin cancer over the past decades is strongly related to increased exposure to the sun during outdoor activities and to artificial sources of UV radiation such as sunlamps and tanning beds. Overexposure is also the underlying cause of harmful effects on the eyes and immune system. Adopting a few simple precautions can greatly reduce the risk of these health conditions: Limit time in the midday sun from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m., particularly on days when the UV index is high When UV rays are most intense and you must be outside, try to find shade and wear protective clothing, including a brimmed hat that provides sun protection for your head, face and neck.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Zulu document:

**To the people, the increase in UV exposure can cause skin cancer, cataracts, and damage to the body's tissues. Prevention:** The rise in the number of skin cancer patients in recent decades is largely linked to sun exposure during outdoor activities and the use of UV-emitting devices such as tanning beds and sun lamps. Excessive sun exposure is also a major cause of harmful effects on the eyes and body tissues. Adopting a few simple safety measures can significantly reduce the risk of these health conditions: Reduce time in the sun during peak hours from 10 am to 4 pm, especially on days when the UV index is high. When UV rays are strongest and you must be outdoors, try to find shade and wear protective clothing, including a hat with a brim that provides sun protection for your head, face, and neck.

translations.source: Sebenzisa isikrini sokuvikela ilanga esino-spectrum obanzi okungenani esingu-15 SPF – ngokuhle kakhulu– futhi uphinde ufake isicelo njalo emahoreni ama-2. Gwema amalambu elanga kanye nezindawo zokushuka isikhumba, njengoba zaziwa ngokulimaza isikhumba namehlo. Ukuchayeka kwe-UV kwezingane kufanele kukhawulwe ngokucophelela ngoba zisesimweni sokukhula futhi ngenxa yalokho zisengozini enkulu ezingozini zemvelo kunabantu abadala. Imisebenzi eminingi ebalulekile efana namasosha omzimba ayikakhuli ngokugcwele lapho ezalwa, futhi izindawo ezingaphephile zingaphazamisa ukukhula kwazo okuvamile. Impendulo yeWHO: IWHO isebenza Namazwe Angamalungu kanye nabalingani ukukhulisa ukuqonda komphakathi ngemiphumela yokuchayeka ngokweqile emisebeni ye-ultraviolet.
translations.reference: Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen of at least 15 SPF – ideally higher– and reapply every 2 hours. Avoid sunlamps and tanning parlours, as they are known to damage the skin and eyes. Children’s UV exposure should be carefully limited because they are in a dynamic state of growth and therefore more susceptible to environmental threats than adults. Many vital functions such as the immune system are not fully developed at birth, and unsafe environments may interfere with their normal development. WHO Response: WHO works with Member States and partners to increase public understanding of the effects of overexposure to ultraviolet radiation.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu text:

"Use a sunscreen with a broad-spectrum protection factor of at least 15 SPF – the higher, the better – and reapply every two hours. Avoid direct sunlight and reflective surfaces, as they are known to damage skin and eyes. Children's exposure to UV rays should be carefully limited because they are in a developmental stage and therefore more vulnerable to environmental hazards than adults. Many important bodily functions are not fully developed at birth, and harmful exposures can disrupt their normal development. WHO response: WHO works with Member States and partners to increase public understanding of the effects of excessive exposure to ultraviolet rays."

translations.source: Uhlelo lwe-INTERSUN luwukubambisana phakathi kweWHO, i-United Nations Environment Programme, iWorld Meteorological Organization, i-International Agency yeCancer Research kanye neKhomishini Yamazwe Ngamazwe Yokuvikela iNon-ionizing Radiation. Ikhuthaza futhi ihlole ucwaningo ngemiphumela yezempilo yemisebe ye-UV, futhi ithuthukise impendulo efanele ngokusebenzisa imihlahlandlela, izincomo kanye nokusabalalisa ulwazi. Imigomo yalolu hlelo kuhlanganise nokunikeza iseluleko esisebenzayo nesiphusile mayelana nemithelela yezempilo nemvelo yokuchayeka kwe-UV, ukukhuthaza amazwe ukuthi athathe isinyathelo sokunciphisa izingozi zezempilo ezibangelwa yi-UV kanye nokunikeza isiqondiso kuziphathimandla zikazwelonke mayelana nezinhlelo zokuqwashisa ngelanga. Kuze kube manje, lolu hlelo lusebenzisana nochwepheshe nama-ejensi angochwepheshe ukwenza imisebenzi ebalulekile yocwaningo, luhlonze futhi lulinganise ubungozi bezempilo emisebeni ye-UV, luthuthukise izibikezelo ezinokwethenjelwa zemiphumela yezempilo neyemvelo yezinguquko zokuchayeka kwe-UV ngokuncipha kwe-ozone yeStratospheric, futhi lithuthukise izindlela ezingokoqobo ukuqapha ushintsho emiphumeleni yezempilo ebangelwa yi-UV ngokuhamba kwesikhathi. Lolu hlelo luhlinzeka ngamathuluzi neziqondiso ezihlobene nenkomba ye-UV, ukuvikela ilanga ezinganeni, ama-sunbed okwenziwa, ezokuvakasha kanye nempilo yomsebenzi.
translations.reference: The INTERSUN Programme is a collaboration between WHO, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization, the International Agency on Cancer Research and the International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. It promotes and evaluates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and develops an appropriate response through guidelines, recommendations and information dissemination. The goals of the programme including providing practical and sound advice on the health and environmental impacts of UV exposure, to encourage countries to take action to reduce UV-induced health risks and to provide guidance to national authorities about sun awareness programmes. To this end, the programme collaborates with experts and specialist agencies to implement key research activities, identifies and quantifies health risks from UV radiation, develops reliable predictions of health and environmental consequences of changes in UV exposure with stratospheric ozone depletion, and develops practical ways of monitoring change in UV-induced health effects over time. The programme provides tools and guidelines related to the UV index, sun protection for children, artificial sunbeds, tourism and occupational health.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

The INTERSUN program is a collaboration between the World Health Organization (WHO), the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization, the International Agency for Cancer Research, and the International Commission on Non-ionizing Radiation Protection. It promotes and investigates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and develops appropriate responses through guidelines, recommendations, and the dissemination of knowledge. The objectives of this program include providing practical and sound advice on the health and environmental impacts of UV exposure, encouraging countries to take action to reduce the health risks caused by UV radiation, and providing guidance to national authorities on sun awareness programs. To date, this program has worked with experts and non-expert agencies to conduct important research, assess and quantify the health risks of UV radiation, develop reliable projections of the health and environmental effects of UV radiation changes due to stratospheric ozone depletion, and develop practical methods to monitor changes in health effects caused by UV radiation over time. This program provides tools and guidance related to the UV index, sun protection for children, solariums, tourism, and occupational health.

translations.source: Ukuhlinzekwa kwezempilo jikelele Uhlolojikelele: I-Universal health coverage (UHC) isho ukuthi bonke abantu banokufinyelela uhla oluphelele lwezinsizakalo zezempilo ezisezingeni eliphezulu abazidingayo, nini futhi kuphi, ngaphandle kobunzima bezezimali. Ihlanganisa ukuqhubeka okuphelele kwezinsizakalo zezempilo ezibalulekile, kusukela ekukhuthazeni impilo kuya ekuvimbeleni, ukwelashwa, ukuhlunyeleliswa kanye nokunakekelwa kwe-palliative. Ukufeza lesi sithembiso, amazwe adinga ukuba nezinhlelo zezempilo eziqinile, ezisebenza kahle nezilinganayo ezigxile emiphakathini ayisebenzelayo. Ukunakekelwa kwezempilo okuyisisekelo (PHC) kuyindlela ephumelela kakhulu neyonga kakhulu yokufika lapho.
translations.reference: Universal health coverage Overview: Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. It covers the full continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care. To deliver on this promise, countries need to have strong, efficient and equitable health systems that are rooted in the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and cost-efficient way to get there.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

**Universal Health Coverage Overview:** Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to a comprehensive range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without facing financial hardship. It includes a continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation, and palliative care. To fulfill this promise, countries need to have robust, efficient, and equitable health systems that are focused on the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and efficient way to achieve this.

translations.source: Izwe ngalinye linendlela ehlukile yokuzuza i-UHC nokunquma ukuthi yini okufanele ihlanganiselwe ngokusekelwe ezidingweni zabantu bakhona kanye nezinsiza ezikhona. Ukutshala imali ku-PHC kuqinisekisa ukuthi zonke lezo zidingo ziyahlonzwa, zibekwe eqhulwini futhi zisingathwe ngendlela edidiyelwe; ukuthi kukhona abasebenzi bezempilo abaqinile nabahlomile; kanye nokuthi yonke imikhakha yomphakathi ibambe iqhaza ekubhekaneni nezici zemvelo nezenhlalo-mnotho ezithinta impilo nokuphila kahle, okuhlanganisa ukulungiselela, ukuphendula kanye nokululama ezimeni eziphuthumayo. Impendulo yeWHO: Njengesisekelo se-UHC, iWHO incoma ukuhlela kabusha izinhlelo zezempilo kusetshenziswa indlela yokunakekelwa kwezempilo okuyisisekelo okyiprimary health care (PHC). Ukuzuza i-UHC yinto eseqhulwini yeWHO, okuhloswe ngayo ukuthi abantu abayizigidi eziyizinkulungwane ezingu-1 bahlomule ekutholeni usizo lwezempilo emhlabeni wonke ngo-2025. Emazweni anezinhlelo zezempilo ezintekenteke, sigxila osizweni lwezobuchwepheshe ukuze kwakhiwe izikhungo zikazwelonke kanye nokulethwa kwezinsiza ukuze sivale izikhala ezibucayi ezimeni eziphuthumayo.
translations.reference: Every country has a different path to achieving UHC and to decide what to cover based on the needs of their populations and the resources at hand. Investing in PHC ensures that all those needs are identified, prioritized and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a robust and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to confronting the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, responding to and recovering from emergencies. WHO response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a WHO strategic priority, with the goal of 1 billion more people benefitting from universal health coverage by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps in emergencies.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

"Every country has a unique way of achieving Universal Health Coverage (UHC) and determining what should be included based on the needs of its people and the available resources. Investing in Primary Health Care (PHC) ensures that all these needs are recognized, prioritized, and addressed in a structured manner; that there are stable and skilled health workers; and that all sectors of society participate in addressing the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparedness, response, and recovery from emergencies. WHO's Response: As a foundation for UHC, the WHO recommends reorienting health systems using the primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a priority for the WHO, with the goal of enabling one billion people to access health care worldwide by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national capacity and deliver resources to close critical gaps in emergency situations."

translations.source: Ezilungiselelweni eziqinile zesistimu yezempilo, siqhuba umthelela wezempilo womphakathi ekuhlinzekeni impilo yabo bonke ngezingxoxo zenqubomgomo yezinhlelo zesikhathi esizayo kanye nokwesekwa kwamasu okwenza ngcono ukusebenza. Lo msebenzi usekelwa isiqondiso esivamile nezivumelwano; idatha, ucwaningo kanye nokusungula izinto ezintsha; kanye nobuholi emikhakheni yezokuxhumana, ukumela, ukulingana ngokobulili, ukulingana kwezempilo kanye namalungelo abantu, isenzo semikhakha eminingi, kanye nezezimali. I-Ajenda ka-2030 yama-SDGs Umsebenzi weWHO uhambisana nezinjongo zeSustainable Development Goals (SDG) ezihlosiwe 3.8, ezigxile ekuzuzeni usizo lwezempilo emhlabeni wonke, okuhlanganisa nokuvikelwa kwengozi yezezimali, ukufinyelela ezinsizeni zokunakekelwa kwezempilo ezibalulekile kanye nokufinyelela ekuphepheni, ngempumelelo, kwekhwalithi nokuthengekayo. imithi ebalulekile kanye nemigomo yawo wonke umuntu. Emhlabeni jikelele, inqubekelaphambili emayelana nokuhlinzekwa kwezempilo kuyinselele. Inqubekelaphambili ekuhlinzekeni ngezinsizakalo imile ngenkathi ingxenye yabantu ababhekene nenhlekelele yokusetshenziswa kwemali kwezempilo ephuma ephaketheni ikhula ngokuqhubekayo.
translations.reference: In more robust health system settings, we drive public health impact towards health coverage for all through policy dialogue for the systems of the future and strategic support to improve performance. This work is supported by normative guidance and agreements; data, research and innovation; and leadership in the realms of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, multisectoral action, and finance. 2030 Agenda for SDGs WHO’s work is aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which focuses on achieving universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage is a challenge. Progress in service coverage has stalled while the proportion of the population facing catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases continuously.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

"Within the framework of robust health system preparations, we are driving the public health impact in providing healthcare to all through policy dialogues for future systems and support for practices that improve efficiency. This work is underpinned by guidance and agreements; data, research, and the development of new things; and leadership in areas of communication, representation, gender equality, health equity, and human rights, multi-sectoral action, and finance. The 2030 Agenda for SDGs The WHO's work aligns with the targeted Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) targets 3.8, focused on achieving universal health coverage, including protection from financial risks, access to essential health services, and access to quality, affordable medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress on health coverage is a challenge. Progress in providing services has stalled while a growing proportion of people face the hardship of out-of-pocket health spending."

translations.source: IWHO iqapha inqubekelaphambili futhi ishicilele imibiko yomhlaba wonke ukuze iveze ubufakazi bakamuva njalo eminyakeni emibili. Bheka imibiko lapha futhi uvakashele ikhasi lamaqiniso e-UHC ukuze uthole ulwazi olwengeziwe.
translations.reference: WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC fact sheet for more information.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the Zulu document:

"WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to present the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC facts page for more information."

translations.source: Impilo yasemadolobheni Uhlolojikelele: Ukufudukela emadolobheni kungenye yezindlela ezihamba phambili emhlabeni wonke zekhulu lama-21 ezinomthelela omkhulu kwezempilo. Bangaphezu kwama-55% abantu bomhlaba abahlala ezindaweni zasemadolobheni - ingxenye okulindeleke ukuthi yenyuke ifinyelele ku-68% ngo-2050. Njengoba ukwanda kwamadolobha amaningi esikhathini esizayo kuzokwenzeka emazweni asathuthuka, umhlaba namuhla unethuba eliyingqayizivele lokuqondisa ukufudukela kwabantu emadolobheni kanye nezinye izindlela ezinkulu zokuthuthukiswa kwamadolobha ngendlela evikela futhi ikhuthaze impilo. Lokhu kubalulekile, hhayi okungenani ngoba impilo nokuphila kahle kwezakhamizi mhlawumbe kuyimpahla ebaluleke kakhulu yedolobha.
translations.reference: Urban health Overview: Urbanization is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century that has a significant impact on health. Over 55% of the world’s population live in urban areas – a proportion that is expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most future urban growth will take place in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development trends in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps a city’s most important asset.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

**Urban Health Overview: Urbanization is one of the major global trends of the 21st century with a significant impact on health. More than 55% of the world's population lives in urban areas – a proportion expected to rise to 68% by 2050. As the growth of many cities in the future will occur in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development pathways in a way that protects and promotes health. This is crucial, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps the most valuable asset of a city.**

translations.source: Nokho, iningi labantu abayizigidi eziyizinkulungwane ezingu-4,2 abahlala emadolobheni alinazo izindlu nezinto zokuhamba ezintula, ukukhucululwa kwendle nokungaphathwa kahle kwemfucuza, kanye nezinga lomoya elizephula iziqondiso zeWHO. Ezinye izinhlobo zokungcola, ezinjengomsindo, ukungcoliswa kwamanzi nomhlabathi, lokho okubizwa ngokuthi iziqhingi zokushisa zasemadolobheni, kanye nokuntuleka kwendawo yokuhamba, ukugibela amabhayisikili nokuphila okumatasa kuhlanganisa ukwenza amadolobha abe yisizinda sesifo esiwumqedazwe wezifo ezingathathelwana kanye nezisusa zokushintsha kwesimo sezulu. IWHO Repository on Urban Health Umthelela: Cishe u-40% wokukhula kwedolobha usemijondolo engenawo amanzi aphephile nezindlu zangasese, kanti abantu abangu-91% basemadolobheni baphefumula umoya ongcolile. Uma kukhulunywa ngezidlo ezinempilo, ukufudukela kwabantu emadolobheni kukhulisa ibanga ukusuka epulazini kuye kwelinye, kushayela isidingo sokudla okungenampilo, okugayiwe.
translations.reference: However, most of the 4.2 billion people living in cities suffer inadequate housing and transport, poor sanitation and waste management, and air quality that fails WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, so-called urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling and active living further combine to make cities epicentres of a noncommunicable disease epidemic and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Around 40% of urban growth is in slums that lack safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to fork, driving demand for unhealthy, processed foods.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

"However, the majority of the 4.2 billion people living in cities lack adequate housing and transportation, face environmental degradation and poor waste management, and experience air quality that violates WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, what is known as urban heat islands, and a lack of transportation space, cycling restrictions, and hectic living conditions contribute to cities becoming a breeding ground for diseases and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Approximately 40% of urban growth occurs in slums without access to safe water and private sanitation, and 91% of urban dwellers breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to table, fueling the demand for unhealthy, processed foods."

translations.source: Abantu abahlala emadolobheni basengozini enkulu yokushintsha kwesimo sezulu ngenxa yokuthembela kwabo ezintweni ezimbiwa phansi zokuthutha, ukupheka kanye nokushisisa. Amadolobhakazi athatha ngaphezu kwezingxenye ezimbili kwezintathu zamandla omhlaba futhi akhipha ama-60% amagesi abamba ukushisa, futhi lawo amaphakathi nezwe angase abe nezinga lokushisa elingu-3–5º C ngaphezu kwezindawo zasemaphandleni ezizungezile ngenxa yobukhulu bazo obukhonkolo kanye nezindawo eziluhlaza ezivulekile ezilinganiselwe. Konke lokhu kubeka impilo yabantu abahlala emadolobheni engcupheni. Iningi lezimbangela eziyishumi eziphezulu zokufa zihlobene eduze nokukhula ngokushesha nokungahleliwe kwedolobha, kanye nokuklanywa nokungahlelwa kahle kwamadolobha. Amadolobha abhekene nomthwalo wezempilo ophindwe kathathu wezifo ezithathelwanayo njengeHIV/AIDS, isifo sofuba, inyumoniya, udenga kanye nesifo sohudo;
translations.reference: Urban dwellers are also highly vulnerable to the effects of climate change because of their dependence on fossil fuels for transport, cooking and heating. Cities account for over two thirds of the world’s energy and emit 60% of greenhouse gases, and those inland may experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas because of their large expanses of concrete and limited open green spaces. All of this puts urban dwellers’ health at risk. Most the top 10 causes of death are closely related to rapid and unplanned urbanization, and poor urban design and planning. Cities face the triple health burden of infectious diseases like HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, dengue and diarrhoea;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

"People living in urban areas are at great risk from climate change due to their dependence on fossil fuels for transportation, cooking, and heating. Cities consume over two-thirds of the world's energy and produce 60% of greenhouse gases, and these urban centers may experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas due to their dense size and limited green spaces. All of this puts the health of urban dwellers at risk. Many of the top ten causes of death are linked to rapid and unplanned urban growth, as well as poorly planned urban design. Cities face a threefold burden of infectious diseases such as HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, influenza, and respiratory diseases."

translations.source: izifo ezingathathelaniyo ezifana nesifo senhliziyo, unhlangothi, isifuba somoya, umdlavuza, isifo sikashukela kanye nokudangala; futhi udlame nokulimala, okuhlanganisa nokulimala emgwaqeni. Impendulo yeWHO: Naphezu kwezinselele zawo, amadolobha angakha amathuba empilo engcono, indawo ehlanzekile kanye nokusebenza kwesimo sezulu. Izinqubomgomo eziqinile zasemadolobheni kufanele zibeke ezempilo kuqala, njengoba ibalulekile ekukhuthazeni impilo enhle yasemadolobheni, ukwakha abasebenzi abakhiqizayo, ukudala imiphakathi eqinile nenempilo, evumela ukuhamba, ukukhuthaza ukuxhumana nomphakathi kanye nokuvikela abantu abasengozini. Indlela yeWHO yezempilo yasemadolobheni igxile eqophelweni eliphezulu lomoya, amanzi kanye nokukhucululwa kwendle, kanye nezinye izici zemvelo; ukuhlela kahle kwedolobha; izindawo ezinempilo nezingabhemi; ukuhamba okuphephile nokunempilo; ukuvimbela udlame nokulimala; ukukhuthaza izinhlelo zokudla okunempilo nokudla okunempilo; ukuphathwa kwemvelo kwezifo ezithwalwa yizilwane; kanye nokulungela amadolobha ezimeni eziphuthumayo.
translations.reference: noncommunicable diseases like heart disease, stroke, asthma, cancer, diabetes and depression; and violence and injuries, including road traffic injuries. WHO response: Despite their challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments and climate action. Strong urban policies must prioritize health, as it is essential for fostering good urban livelihoods, building a productive workforce, creating resilient and vibrant communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO’s approach to urban health focuses on better air quality, water and sanitation, and other environmental determinants; healthy urban planning; healthier and smoke-free environments; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy food systems and diets; environmental management of vector-borne diseases; and urban preparedness for emergencies.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

"Non-communicable diseases such as heart disease, stroke, respiratory disease, cancer, diabetes, and asthma; and violence and injury, including road traffic accidents. WHO response: Despite its challenges, cities can build opportunities for better health, clean environments, and climate action. Strong urban policies should prioritize health, as it is essential for promoting urban well-being, building a productive workforce, creating strong and healthy communities, enabling mobility, fostering social connections, and protecting vulnerable people. The WHO’s approach to urban health focuses on air quality, water, and sanitation, as well as other environmental factors; urban planning; healthy and safe spaces; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injury; promoting healthy food systems and nutrition; managing zoonotic diseases; and preparing cities for emergencies."

translations.source: Ukusebenza kuyo yonke imikhakha kanye nokuqinisekisa ukuhambisana kwezinqubomgomo kuzo zonke izindawo ezihlukene kubalulekile ekudaleni izindawo ezisekelayo nezivumelayo zezempilo, okuqinisekisa ukuthi ukucatshangelwa kwezempilo nokulingana kuyahlanganiswa phakathi nenqubo yokuhlela, ukutshalwa kwezimali kanye nezinqumo zenqubomgomo ezingeni lendawo. Ukusiza Amazwe Angamalungu ukubhekana nalezi zinto ezibalulekile ezingenhla, iWHO isebenza ukuqinisa isisekelo sobufakazi ukuze isize abenzi bezinqubomgomo benze izinqumo ezinolwazi lapho bebhekana nezingozi zezempilo. Ihlinzeka ngamathuluzi nesiqondiso kulokho okusebenzayo, futhi isekela ukubhekwa kwezinkomba ezibalulekile ezihlobene nempilo. IWHO ihola futhi izibandakanye ekukhuthazeni ukuhwebelana kwedolobha nedolobha futhi isiza ukuthuthukisa izinhlaka zesikhungo nenqubomgomo yokuphatha okuhle kwedolobha kwezempilo nokuphila kahle emadolobheni.
translations.reference: Working across sectors and ensuring the coherence of policies across different areas is key to creating supportive and enabling environments for health, which ensures that health and equity considerations are integrated throughout the planning process, investments and policy decisions at the local level. To help Member States address the above priorities, WHO works to strengthen the evidence base to help policy-makers make informed decisions when addressing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports monitoring of key health-related indicators. WHO leads and engages in fostering city-to-city exchanges and helps develop institutional and policy frameworks for good urban governance for health and well-being in cities.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

"Working across all sectors and ensuring policy alignment in different settings is crucial in creating supportive and enabling health environments, ensuring that health considerations and equity are integrated throughout the planning, investment, and policy-making process at the local level. To assist Member States in addressing the above critical issues, the WHO is working to strengthen the evidence base to help policymakers make informed decisions when facing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports the monitoring of key health-related indicators. The WHO also leads and engages in promoting city-to-city collaboration and assists in developing institutional frameworks and policy governance for good health and well-being in cities."

translations.source: Udlame olubhekiswe ezinganeni Uhlolojikelele: Udlame olubhekiswe ezinganeni luhlanganisa zonke izinhlobo zodlame olubhekiswe kubantu abangaphansi kweminyaka engu-18 ubudala. Ezinsaneni nasezinganeni ezincane, udlame luhilela ikakhulukazi ukuphathwa kabi kwezingane (okungukuthi, ukuhlukunyezwa ngokomzimba, ngokocansi nangokomzwelo kanye nokunganakwa) ezandleni zabazali nabanye abaphethe. Abafana namantombazane basengozini elinganayo yokuhlukunyezwa ngokomzimba nangokomzwelo nokunganakwa, futhi amantombazane asengozini enkulu yokuhlukunyezwa ngokocansi. Njengoba izingane zifinyelela eminyakeni yobusha, udlame lontanga nodlame olusondelene nozakwethu, ngaphezu kokuphathwa kabi kwezingane, kuyanda.
translations.reference: Violence against children Overview: Violence against children includes all forms of violence against people under 18 years old. For infants and younger children, violence mainly involves child maltreatment (i.e. physical, sexual and emotional abuse and neglect) at the hands of parents and other authority figures. Boys and girls are at equal risk of physical and emotional abuse and neglect, and girls are at greater risk of sexual abuse. As children reach adolescence, peer violence and intimate partner violence, in addition to child maltreatment, become highly prevalent.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

**Child Maltreatment**

**Overview:** Child maltreatment encompasses all forms of abuse against individuals under the age of 18. In children and young children, maltreatment primarily involves the mistreatment of children (that is, physical, sexual, and emotional abuse and neglect) in the hands of parents or other caregivers. Boys and girls are at equal risk of physical, emotional abuse, and neglect, while girls are at greater risk of sexual abuse. As children reach adolescence, peer violence and dating violence, in addition to child abuse, increase.

translations.source: Udlame olubhekiswe ezinganeni lungagwemeka. Ukuvimbela nokusabela odlameni olubhekiswe ezinganeni kudinga ukuthi imizamo ibhekane nengozi kanye nezici zokuzivikela kuwo wonke amazinga amane ahlobene obungozi (umuntu ngamunye, ubudlelwano, umphakathi, umphakathi). Isinqumo soMhlangano Womhlaba Wezempilo ka-Nhlaba 2016 sagunyaza uhlelo lokuqala lweWHO Lokusebenza Lomhlaba wonke lokuqinisa indima yohlelo lwezempilo ngaphakathi kwempendulo yemikhakha ehlukahlukene kazwelonke yokubhekana nodlame phakathi kwabantu, ikakhulukazi kwabesifazane namantombazane, kanye nezingane. A Ngokwalolu hlelo, iWHO ngokubambisana Namazwe Angamalungu kanye nabanye ababambisene nabo, izibophezele: Ukuqapha ubukhulu bomhlaba wonke kanye nezici zodlame olubhekiswe ezinganeni kanye nokusekela imizamo yezwe yokubhala kanye nokukala ubudlova obunjalo. Ukugcina isistimu yolwazi lwe-elekthronikhi efingqa idatha yesayensi yomthwalo, izici engcuphe kanye nemiphumela yodlame olubhekiswe ezinganeni, kanye nobufakazi bokuvikeleka kwalo.
translations.reference: Violence against children can be prevented. Preventing and responding to violence against children requires that efforts systematically address risk and protective factors at all four interrelated levels of risk (individual, relationship, community, society). May 2016 World Health Assembly resolution endorsed the first ever WHO Global plan of action on strengthening the role of the health system within a national multisectoral response to address interpersonal violence, in particular against women and girls, and against children. A According to this plan, WHO in collaboration with Member States and other partners, is committed to: Monitoring the global magnitude and characteristics of violence against children and supporting country efforts to document and measure such violence. Maintaining an electronic information system that summarizes the scientific data on the burden, risk factors and consequences of violence against children, and the evidence for its preventability.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

"Child abuse is preventable. Preventing and responding to child abuse requires efforts to address the risks and protective factors at all four levels related to risk (individual, relationship, community, society). The resolution of the World Health Assembly in May 2016 endorsed the WHO’s first Global Plan of Action to strengthen the role of the health sector within multi-sectoral national responses to interpersonal violence, particularly against women and girls, and children. According to this plan, WHO, in partnership with Member States and other partners, is committed to: Monitoring the global extent and characteristics of child abuse and supporting national efforts to report and measure such violence. Maintaining a robust electronic information system that captures scientific data on the burden, risk factors, and consequences of child abuse, and evidence of its prevention."

translations.source: Ukuthuthukisa nokusabalalisa imibhalo yeziqondiso zezobuchwepheshe ezisekelwe ebufakazini, izinkambiso nezindinganiso zokuvimbela kanye nokusabela kubudlova obubhekiswe ezinganeni. Ukushicilela njalo imibiko yesimo somhlaba ngemizamo yezwe yokubhekana nodlame olubhekiswe ezinganeni ngezinqubomgomo zikazwelonke nezinhlelo zokwenza, imithetho, izinhlelo zokuvimbela kanye nezinsizakalo zokuphendula. Amazwe asekelayo nabalingani ekusebenziseni amasu okuvimbela nokuphendula asekelwe ebufakazini, njengalawo afakwe ku-INSPIRE: Amasu ayisikhombisa okuqeda udlame olubhekiswe ezinganeni. Ukusebenzisana nama-ejensi nezinhlangano zamazwe ngamazwe ukunciphisa nokuqeda udlame olubhekiswe ezinganeni emhlabeni wonke, ngezinhlelo ezifana ne-Global Partnership Yokuqeda Udlame Olubhekiswe Kubantwana, IGlobal Partnership Ukuqeda Udlame Olubhekiswe Kubantwana,
translations.reference: Developing and disseminating evidence-based technical guidance documents, norms and standards for preventing and responding to violence against children. Regularly publishing global status reports on country efforts to address violence against children through national policies and action plans, laws, prevention programmes and response services. Supporting countries and partners in implementing evidence-based prevention and response strategies, such as those included in INSPIRE: Seven strategies for ending violence against children. Collaborating with international agencies and organizations to reduce and eliminate violence against children globally, through initiatives such as the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children,
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

"To develop and disseminate technical guidance materials based on evidence, standards, and guidelines for preventing and responding to violence against children. To regularly publish reports on the state of the world regarding national efforts to address violence against children through national policies and action plans, laws, prevention programs, and response services. To support countries and partners in using evidence-based prevention and response strategies, such as those included in INSPIRE: 8 Strategies for Ending Violence Against Children. translations.source: Sihlangene Samantombazane kanye neViolence Prevention Alliance. Ukuphathwa kabi kwezingane Ukuhlukunyezwa kwezingane wukuhlukunyezwa nokunganakwa okwenzeka ezinganeni ezingaphansi kweminyaka eyi-18. Kuhlanganisa zonke izinhlobo zokuphathwa kabi ngokomzimba kanye/noma ngokomzwelo, ukuhlukunyezwa ngokocansi, ukunganakwa, ubudedengu nokuxhashazwa kwezentengiselwano noma okunye, okuholela ekulimaleni kwangempela noma okungaba khona empilweni yengane, ekusindeni kwayo, ekukhuleni kwayo noma ekuhloniphekeni kwayo esimweni sobudlelwano umthwalo wemfanelo, ukwethenjwa noma amandla. Ukuchayeka kudlame lozakwethu kwesinye isikhathi kufakwa njengendlela yokuphathwa kabi kwezingane.
translations.reference: Together for Girls and the Violence Prevention Alliance. Child maltreatment Child maltreatment is the abuse and neglect that occurs to children under 18 years of age. It includes all types of physical and/or emotional ill-treatment, sexual abuse, neglect, negligence and commercial or other exploitation, which results in actual or potential harm to the child’s health, survival, development or dignity in the context of a relationship of responsibility, trust or power. Exposure to intimate partner violence is also sometimes included as a form of child maltreatment.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

We are partnering with the Young Women's Organisation and the Violence Prevention Alliance. Child Maltreatment Child abuse is the abuse and neglect experienced by children under the age of 18. It includes all forms of physical and/or emotional mistreatment, sexual abuse, neglect, exploitation, trafficking or any other, leading to actual or potential harm in a child's life, their survival, their development or their dignity in the context of relationships, trust or power. Exposure to domestic violence is sometimes included as a form of child maltreatment.

translations.source: Ukuphathwa kabi kwezingane kunemiphumela emikhulu esheshayo neyesikhathi eside. Ngaphandle kokufa, ukulimala ngokomzimba nokukhubazeka, udlame lungaholela ekucindezelekeni okuphazamisa ukukhula kobuchopho futhi kulimaze amasosha omzimba kanye nemizwa. Lokhu kubuye kuhlotshaniswe nokubambezeleka kokuthuthukiswa kwengqondo, ukungaphumeleli kahle kwesikole kanye nokuyeka isikole, izinkinga zempilo yengqondo, ukuzama ukuzibulala, ukwanda kokuziphatha okuyingozi empilweni, ukuvuselelwa kanye nokubhebhezelwa kodlame. Izindaba ezinhle ukuthi ukuhlukunyezwa kwezingane kungavinjelwa ngokungenelela okusekela abazali nabanakekeli, ukukhuthaza imikhuba nezindinganiso ezingenalo ubudlova, ukuhlinzeka ngemfundo nokuqeqeshwa kwamakhono okuphila, ukuqinisa iholo lemindeni kanye nokuvikeleka kwezomnotho, ukunikeza impendulo yezinga eliphezulu kanye nezinsizakalo zokusekela, ukudala. kanye nokusimamisa izindawo eziphephile zezingane, nokusebenzisa nokuphoqelela imithetho emelene nokuphathwa kabi kwezingane. IWHO, ngokubambisana nenani lozakwethu:
translations.reference: Child maltreatment has enormous immediate and long-term repercussions. Beyond death, physical injury and disability, violence can lead to stress that impairs brain development and damages the nervous and immune systems. This in turn is associated with delayed cognitive development, poor school performance and dropout, mental health problems, suicide attempts, increased health-risk behaviours, revictimization and the perpetration of violence. The good news is that child maltreatment can be prevented through interventions that support parents and caregivers, promote non-violent norms and values, provide education and life skills training, strengthen families’ income and economic security, offer high quality response and support services, create and sustain safe environments for children, and implement and enforce laws against child maltreatment. WHO, in collaboration with a number of partners:
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

"The mistreatment of children has significant and long-lasting immediate and long-term effects. Apart from death, physical injury and disability, violence can lead to stress that disrupts brain development and damages physical and emotional health. This is also linked to delays in intellectual development, poor school performance and dropping out of school, mental health problems, attempted suicide, an increase in risky behaviors in life, substance abuse, and the perpetuation of violence. The good news is that child abuse can be prevented through interventions that support parents and caregivers, promoting practices and norms free of violence, providing education and life skills training, strengthening family income and economic security, providing quality responses and support services, creating and maintaining safe spaces for children, and implementing and enforcing laws against child abuse. WHO, in partnership with a number of partners:"

translations.source: Kubandakanya uhla lwezenzo kusukela ebuxhwanguxhwangwini kanye nokulwa ngokomzimba, ukuya ekuhlaselweni kanzima ngokocansi nangokomzimba kuya ekubulaleni. Udlame lwentsha lunyusa kakhulu izindleko zezempilo, inhlalakahle kanye nobulungiswa bobugebengu; kunciphisa ukukhiqiza; futhi ngokuvamile ibukela phansi isisekelo somphakathi. Ngaphandle kokufa, ukulimala kanye nokulimala kwengqondo, udlame lwentsha lungaholela ekwandeni kokuziphatha okuyingozi kwezempilo njengokubhema, ukusebenzisa kabi izidakamizwa, ucansi olungaphephile, kanye nobudlova obengeziwe. Lokhu-ke kuhlotshaniswa nezifo ezingelapheki zokuphefumula, umdlavuza, izifo zenhliziyo, isifo sikashukela, ukukhulelwa besebancane kanye nezifo ezithathelwana ngocansi. Amasu asebenzayo okuvimbela kanye nokusabela ahlanganisa lawo akhuthaza ukuba umzali kanye nokuthuthukiswa kwengane isencane;
translations.reference: It includes a range of acts from bullying and physical fighting, to more severe sexual and physical assault to homicide. Youth violence dramatically increases health, welfare and criminal justice costs; reduces productivity; and generally undermines the fabric of society. Beyond deaths, injuries and psychological harm, youth violence can lead to increased health risk behaviours such as smoking, substance abuse, unsafe sex, and further violence. These in turn are associated with chronic respiratory diseases, cancer, cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, early pregnancy and sexually transmitted diseases. Effective prevention and response strategies include those that promote parenting and early childhood development;
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu text:

"It encompasses a range of actions from bullying and physical fights, to severe sexual and physical assault, all the way to murder. Youth violence significantly increases healthcare costs, social welfare, and criminal justice expenses; it reduces productivity; and often undermines the social fabric. Apart from death, physical injuries and mental trauma, youth violence can lead to an increase in risky health behaviors such as smoking, substance abuse, unsafe sex, and increased crime. This is associated with incurable respiratory diseases, cancer, heart disease, HIV/AIDS, teenage pregnancy, and sexually transmitted infections. Effective prevention and response strategies include those that promote parenting and early childhood development."

translations.source: izinhlelo zokuvimbela ubuxhwanguxhwangu ezikoleni, ezifundweni, kanye nezokuthuthukisa amakhono empilo nezenhlalo; izindlela zokwelapha ezinabantu abasengozini enkulu, kanye nezindlela ezisezingeni lomphakathi kanye nomphakathi ezifana nokunciphisa ukufinyelela kanye nokusetshenziswa budedengu kotshwala nezibhamu, izindawo ezishisayo kanye namaphoyisa agxile ezinkingeni, ukuthuthukisa amadolobha, kanye nokuqeda ubumpofu. IWHO kanye nozakwethu banciphisa udlame lwentsha ngezinyathelo ezisiza ukuhlonza, ukulinganisa nokuphendula inkinga, lokhu kufaka phakathi: ukwakha iphakethe lezinhlelo zokuvimbela udlame ezikoleni; idonsela ukunaka ubukhulu bobudlova bentsha kanye nesidingo sokuvimbela;
translations.reference: school-based bullying prevention, academic, and life and social skills development programmes; therapeutic approaches with high-risk individuals, and community- and society-level approaches like reducing access to and misuse of alcohol and firearms, hotspots and problem-oriented policing, urban upgrading, and poverty de-concentration. WHO and partners are reducing youth violence through measures that help identify, equalize, and respond to the problem, including: building a package of violence prevention programs in schools; highlighting the extent of youth crime and the need for prevention."

translations.source: Udlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane Uhlolojikelele: Udlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane - ikakhulukazi udlame olusondelene nabalingani kanye nodlame locansi - yinkinga enkulu yezempilo yomphakathi neyezempilo kanye nokwephulwa kwamalungelo abantu besifazane. Isekelwe futhi iqhubekisela phambili ukungalingani kobulili. Emhlabeni wonke owesifazane oyedwa kwabathathu uba nodlame olungokomzimba kanye/noma locansi ekuphileni kwabo, kakhulukazi okwenziwa umlingani osondelene naye. Lesi yisikhumbuzo esiqinile sezinga lokungalingani ngokobulili kanye nokucwaswa kwabantu besifazane. I-United Nations ichaza udlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane “njenganoma yisiphi isenzo sodlame olusekelwe ebulilini esiholela, noma esingase sibangele, ukulimala ngokomzimba, ngokocansi, noma kwengqondo noma ukuhlupheka kwabesifazane, okuhlanganisa izinsongo zezenzo ezinjalo, ukuphoqelelwa noma ukuncishwa okuthile ngokungafanele. inkululeko, kungakhathaliseki ukuthi yenzeka emphakathini noma empilweni yangasese.
translations.reference: Violence against women Overview: Violence against women – particularly intimate partner violence and sexual violence – is a major public and clinical health problem and a violation of women's human rights. It is rooted in and perpetuates gender inequalities.Globally 1 in 3 women experience physical and/or sexual violence in their lifetime, mostly by an intimate partner. This is a stark reminder of the scale of gender inequality and discrimination against women. The United Nations defines violence against women as "any act of gender-based violence that results in, or is likely to result in, physical, sexual, or mental harm or suffering to women, including threats of such acts, coercion or arbitrary deprivation of liberty, whether occurring in public or in private life.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

**Violence Against Women: An Overview**

Violence against women – particularly intimate partner violence and sexual violence – is a major public health and health issue, as well as a violation of women's human rights. It is rooted in and perpetuated by gender inequality. Worldwide, one in three women experience physical and/or sexual violence in their lifetime, most often at the hands of an intimate partner. This is a stark reminder of the level of gender inequality and the marginalization of women. The United Nations defines violence against women as “any act of violence against women that results in, or is likely to result in, physical, sexual, or psychological harm or suffering to women, including threats of such acts, coercion or arbitrary deprivation of liberty, whether occurring in public or in private.”

translations.source: Udlame lozakwethu abasondelene lusho ukuziphatha komlingani osondelene naye noma owake wathandana naye okubangela ukulimala ngokomzimba, ngokocansi noma ngokwengqondo, okuhlanganisa ulaka olungokomzimba, ukucindezelwa ngokocansi, ukuhlukumeza ngokomqondo kanye nokuziphatha okulawulayo. Udlame locansi "yinoma yisiphi isenzo socansi, umzamo wokuthola isenzo socansi, noma esinye isenzo esiqondiswe ngokumelene nobulili bomuntu kusetshenziswa ukuphoqwa, yinoma yimuphi umuntu ngokunganaki ubudlelwano bakhe nomuntu ohlukunyeziwe, kunoma yisiphi isimo. Kuhlanganisa ukudlwengulwa, okuchazwa njengokungena ngenkani ngokomzimba noma ngenye indlela kwengquza noma ididi ngepipi, enye ingxenye yomzimba noma into." Umthelela: Udlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane luyinkinga yomhlaba wonke yokwanda kobhubhane. Kudala umonakalo, ukulimala ezimpilweni zabesifazane kanye nezabantwana babo.
translations.reference: Intimate partner violence refers to behaviour by an intimate partner or ex-partner that causes physical, sexual or psychological harm, including physical aggression, sexual coercion, psychological abuse and controlling behaviours. Sexual violence is "any sexual act, attempt to obtain a sexual act, or other act directed against a person’s sexuality using coercion, by any person regardless of their relationship to the victim, in any setting. It includes rape, defined as the physically forced or otherwise coerced penetration of the vulva or anus with a penis, other body part or object." Impact: Violence against women is a global problem of pandemic proportions. It causes devastating, harm to women’s lives and that of their children.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

"Intimate partner violence (IPV) refers to the behavior of an intimate partner or former romantic partner that causes physical, sexual, or emotional harm, including physical assault, sexual coercion, emotional abuse, and controlling behavior. Sexual violence is defined as "any sexual act, attempt to obtain a sexual act, or other act committed against a person’s sexual autonomy, using force, or any other form of coercion; or any act that denies a person their right to refuse sexual advances, or any situation." It includes rape, defined as "any penetration of the vagina, anus, or other body parts, whether by a penis, another body part, or an object." Impact: Violence against women is a global problem contributing to increased mortality rates. It causes damage and harm to the health of women and their children."

**Key notes on the translation:**

*   I aimed for clarity and accuracy while maintaining a formal tone appropriate for the subject matter.
*   I used standard English terminology for concepts like "intimate partner violence" and "sexual violence."
*   I preserved the direct quotes from the original text.
*   I tried to capture the nuances of the Zulu language as best as possible within the constraints of English.

translations.source: Kulimaza futhi impilo yezomnotho nenhlalo yemindeni yabo, imiphakathi, kanye namazwe. Nakuba abanye besifazane besengozini kakhulu kunabanye, udlame lungenzeka kunoma yimuphi umuntu wesifazane, kunoma yiliphi izwe - kungakhathaliseki isiko, inkolo noma isimo somnotho. Ukungalingani ngokobulili okuhlanganisa nezinkambiso zobulili eziyingozi yizona ezihamba phambili zodlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane. Udlame lungaphazamisa impilo yabesifazane ngokomzimba, ngokwengqondo, ocansini, kanye nokuzala. Kuhambisana nokwanda kwengozi yokulimala, ukudangala, ukuphazamiseka kokukhathazeka, ukukhulelwa okungahleliwe, izifo ezithathelwana ngocansi, iHIV nezinye izinkinga eziningi zempilo.
translations.reference: It also hurts the economic and social health of their families, communities, and countries. While some women are more at risk than others, violence can happen to any woman, in any country – regardless of culture, religion or economic status. Gender inequality including harmful gender norms are key drivers of violence against women. Violence can negatively affect women’s physical, mental, sexual, and reproductive health. It is associated with increased risk of injuries, depression, anxiety disorders, unplanned pregnancies, sexually transmitted infections, HIV and many other health problems.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

"It damages the economic well-being and social stability of women, their families, communities, and nations. While some women are at greater risk than others, violence can affect any woman, in any country – regardless of culture, religion, or socioeconomic status. Gender inequality, including harmful gender norms, is a leading cause of violence against women. Violence can disrupt women's physical, mental, emotional, and reproductive health. It is associated with an increased risk of injury, disability, anxiety disorders, unintended pregnancies, sexually transmitted infections, HIV, and many other health problems."

translations.source: Izingane ezikhulela emikhayeni enobudlova zingase zibe nokuphazamiseka okuhlukahlukene kokuziphatha nemizwelo. Lokhu kungaholela ezinkingeni zengqondo nokukhula futhi kuhlotshaniswe nokwenza noma ukubhekana nodlame kamuva ekuphileni. Impendulo yeWHO: iWHO idlala indima ebalulekile ekuletheni ukunaka nokuphendula udlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane njengendaba yezempilo yomphakathi kanye nokulingana kobulili ngokusebenzisa: Ucwaningo nokwakhiwa kobufakazi ukuze kugqanyiswe ubukhulu bodlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane, izici zalo eziyingozi kanye nemiphumela, kanye nokuhlonza ukungenelela okuphumelelayo kokuvimbela nokusabela. Ukuthuthukisa imihlahlandlela namathuluzi, ukubeka izimiso nezindinganiso zokuphendula ngempumelelo kwezempilo kubudlova obubhekiswe kwabesifazane.
translations.reference: Children who grow up in families where there is violence may suffer a range of behavioural and emotional disturbances. These can lead to mental and development problems and also be associated with perpetrating or experiencing violence later in life. WHO response: WHO plays a key role in bringing attention to and responding to violenceagainst women as a public health and gender equality issue through: Research and evidence-building to highlight the magnitude of violence against women, its risk factors and consequences, and to identify effective interventions for prevention and response. Developing guidelines and tools, setting norms and standards for an effective health response to violence against women.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

"Children who grow up in abusive households may experience a variety of behavioral and emotional disturbances. This can lead to mental health problems and developmental issues and is linked to perpetrating or experiencing violence later in life. WHO's Response: WHO plays a crucial role in raising awareness and responding to violence against women as a public health issue and gender equality through: Research and evidence-building to assess the extent of violence against women, its risk factors and consequences, and to identify effective prevention and response interventions. Developing guidelines and tools, setting standards and principles for effectively responding to health issues related to violence against women."

translations.source: Ukuqinisa amandla ezwe wezinhlelo zezempilo nabahlinzeki ukuze kuvinjelwe futhi kuphendule udlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane. Ukukhuthaza ubuholi ezinhlelweni zezempilo kanye nokwakha intando yezepolitiki yokubhekana nodlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane ngokukhulumela nokusebenzisana. iWHO 13th General Programme of Work (GPW) ihlanganisa udlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane njengengxenye yomnikelo walo kuSustainable Development Goal (SDG) 5 mayelana nokulingana kobulili kanye nokufukulwa kwabesifazane (ikakhulukazi, SDG 5.2 mayelana nokuqedwa kodlame olubhekiswe kwabesifazane namantombazane). Emhlanganweni Wezempilo Womhlaba ngoNhlaba 2016, Amazwe Angamalungu agunyaza uhlelo lokusebenza lomhlaba wonke lokuqinisa indima yezinhlelo zezempilo ekubhekaneni nodlame phakathi kwabantu, ikakhulukazi abesifazane namantombazane kanye nezingane.
translations.reference: Strengthening country capacity of health systems and providers to prevent and respond to violence against women. Encouraging leadership in health systems and building political will to address violence against women through advocacy and partnerships. The WHO 13th General Programme of Work (GPW) includes violence against women as part of its contribution to Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 5 on gender equality and women’s empowerment (specifically, SDG 5.2 on the elimination of violence against women and girls). At the World Health Assembly in May 2016, Member States endorsed a global plan of action on strengthening the role of the health systems in addressing interpersonal violence, in particular against women and girls and against children.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the English translation of the provided Zulu document:

"Strengthening the capacity of health systems and healthcare providers to prevent and respond to gender-based violence. Promoting leadership in health systems and building political will to address gender-based violence through advocacy and collaboration. The WHO 13th General Programme of Work (GPW) includes gender-based violence as part of its contribution to Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 5 on gender equality and the empowerment of women (specifically, SDG 5.2 on ending all forms of violence against women and girls). At the World Health Assembly in May 2016, Member States endorsed a global plan of action to strengthen the role of health systems in addressing violence against people, particularly women and girls and children."

translations.source: Imililo yequbula Uhlolojikelele: Imililo yequbula iyanda emhlabeni wonke ngobuningi, ubukhali kanye nobude besikhathi, ikhulisa isidingo sokuqonda imiphumela yezempilo yokuchayeka emlilweni wequbula. Ingozi yomlilo wequbula ikhula ezimeni ezome kakhulu, njengesomiso, amagagasi okushisa nangesikhathi somoya onamandla.Intuthu yomlilo wequbula iyingxube yezinto ezingcolisa umoya eziyingozi, ezifana nePM2.5, NO2, i-ozone, amahydrocarbon anephunga elimnandi, noma umthofu. Ngaphezu kokungcolisa umoya ngezinto ezingcolisayo ezinobuthi, imililo yequbula iphinde ithinte isimo sezulu ngasikhathi sinye ngokukhiphela inqwaba yesikhutha namanye amagesi abamba ukushisa emkhathini. Ngokushintsha kwesimo sezulu okuholela emazingeni okushisa afudumele nezimo ezomile kanye nokwanda kokukhula kwezindawo zasemadolobheni, inkathi yomlilo iqala ngaphambi kwesikhathi futhi iphele kamuva.
translations.reference: Wildfires Overview: Wildfires are increasing around the globe in frequency, severity and duration, heightening the need to understand the health effects of wildfire exposure. The risk of wildfires grows in extremely dry conditions, such as drought, heat waves and during high winds.Wildfire smoke is a mixture of hazardous air pollutants, such PM2.5, NO2, ozone, aromatic hydrocarbons, or lead. In addition to contaminating the air with toxic pollutants, wildfires also simultaneously impact the climate by releasing large quantities of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. With climate change leading to warmer temperatures and drier conditions and the increasing urbanization of rural areas, the fire season is starting earlier and ending later.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the Zulu document into English:

**Wildfire Overview: Wildfires are increasing globally in number, intensity, and duration, highlighting the need to understand the health impacts of wildfire smoke exposure. The risk of wildfire grows in hotter and drier conditions, such as droughts, heat waves, and during periods of strong winds. Wildfire smoke is a mixture of hazardous air pollutants, such as PM2.5, NO2, ozone, volatile organic compounds (VOCs), or soot. In addition to air pollution with harmful pollutants, wildfires also affect the climate simultaneously by releasing large amounts of greenhouse gases and other heat-trapping gases into the atmosphere. With climate change leading to higher temperatures, drier conditions, and the expansion of urban areas, the wildfire season starts earlier and lasts longer.**

translations.source: Imicimbi yomlilo wequbula iba ngokwedlulele ngokwedlulele mayelana nokushiswa kwamahektare, ubude besikhathi namandla, futhi ingaphazamisa ezokuthutha, ukuxhumana, ukuhlinzekwa kwamanzi, namandla kanye nezinsizakalo zegesi. Umthelela: Imililo yequbula evutha eduze kwezindawo ezinabantu abaningi ingaba nomthelela omkhulu endaweni ezungezile, impahla, imfuyo kanye nokufa kwabantu kanye nezifo kuye ngobukhulu, isivinini kanye nokuba seduze komlilo, kanye nokuthi ingabe abantu banesexwayiso esithuthukisiwe sokuthi baphume. Intuthu yomlilo wequbula iyingxube yezinto ezingcolisa umoya okuyiparticulate matter (PM) ewusongo olukhulu lwezempilo yomphakathi. iPM2.5 evela kuntuthu womlilo wequbula ihlotshaniswa nokufa ngaphambi kwesikhathi kubantu abaningi, futhi ingabangela futhi yandise izifo zamaphaphu, inhliziyo, ubuchopho/isimiso sezinzwa, isikhumba, amathumbu, izinso, amehlo, ikhala nesibindi. Kubuye kwaboniswa ukuthi kuholela ekukhubazekeni kwengqondo nokulahlekelwa inkumbulo.
translations.reference: Wildfire events are getting more extreme in terms of acres burned, duration and intensity, and they can disrupt transportation, communications, water supply, and power and gas services. Impact: Wildfires that burn near populated areas can have significant impact on the environment, property, livestock and human mortality and morbidity depending on the size, speed and proximity to the fire, and whether the population has advanced warning to evacuate. Wildfire smoke is a mixture of air pollutants of which particulate matter (PM) is the principal public health threat. PM2.5 from wildfire smoke is associated with premature deaths in the general population, and can cause and exacerbate diseases of the lungs, heart, brain/nervous system, skin, gut, kidney, eyes, nose and liver. It has also been shown to lead to cognitive impairment and memory loss.
translations.hypothesis:
